segments.text: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to His disciples:
The scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So practice and observe everything they tell you. But do not do what they do, for they do not practice what they preach.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They tie up heavy, burdensome loads[^148] and lay them on men’s shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to lift a finger to move them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All their deeds are done for men to see. They broaden their phylacteries and lengthen their tassels.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They love the places of honor at banquets, the chief seats in the synagogues,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: the greetings in the marketplaces, and the title of ‘Rabbi’ by which they are addressed.[^149]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But you are not to be called ‘Rabbi,’ for you have one Teacher, and you are all brothers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And do not call anyone on earth your father, for you have one Father, who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Nor are you to be called instructors, for you have one Instructor, the Christ.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The greatest among you shall be your servant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You shut the kingdom of heaven in men’s faces. You yourselves do not enter, nor will you let in those who wish to enter.[^150]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You traverse land and sea to win a single convert, and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of hell[^151] as you are.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, blind guides! You say, ‘If anyone swears by the temple, it means nothing; but if anyone swears by the gold of the temple, he is bound by his oath.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind fools! Which is greater: the gold, or the temple that makes it sacred?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you say, ‘If anyone swears by the altar, it means nothing; but if anyone swears by the gift on it, he is bound by his oath.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind men! Which is greater: the gift, or the altar that makes it sacred?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So then, he who swears by the altar swears by it and by everything on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he who swears by the temple swears by it and by the One who dwells in it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he who swears by heaven swears by God’s throne and by the One who sits on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You pay tithes of mint, dill, and cumin. But you have disregarded the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faithfulness. You should have practiced the latter, without neglecting the former.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel.[^152]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and dish,[^153] so that the outside may become clean as well.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside, but on the inside are full of dead men’s bones and every kind of impurity.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way, on the outside you appear to be righteous, but on the inside you are full of hypocrisy and wickedness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the monuments of the righteous.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partners with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So you testify against yourselves that you are the sons of those who murdered the prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Fill up, then,[^154] the measure of the sin of your fathers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You snakes! You brood of vipers! How will you escape the sentence of hell?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Because of this, I am sending you prophets and wise men and teachers. Some of them you will kill and crucify, and others you will flog in your synagogues and persecute in town after town.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And so upon you will come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Berechiah, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
Truly I tell you, all these things will come upon this generation.

## Lament over Jerusalem
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were unwilling!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Look, your house is left to you desolate.[^155]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you that you will not see Me again until you say, ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord.’[^156]
segments.speaker: Jesus
